intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible in the yellow pot. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present, anchored by the white rabbit figurine.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos foliage visible, healthy anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no growth detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Soil surface contains debris (eggshell fragments); no viable plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos remains the sole occupant; stable posture.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, appears moist/compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P2/P4/P3 cluster).
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No fungal blooms or moss observed; desk surface is clear.

temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Minor shift in lighting/shadows; plant posture remains unchanged.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Significant reduction in visible light (blue-spectrum shift); plant structure remains static.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'Stasis/Decline'. P3 is the only surviving specimen. The lack of growth in P1, P2, and P4 suggests either dormancy or total loss of the original specimens.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing.
Persistent absence of P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently dominated by a single Pothos specimen (P3). The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively barren of their registered occupants, showing only soil and scattered eggshell debris. The environment is stable but lacks biological diversity as per the registry.
